clusters.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrives in Netherlands
clusters.neutral_headline: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship docks in the Netherlands
bullet_summary: Health officials have identified at least 10 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship
Reported by CBS News, BBC Top Stories, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrives in Netherlands
items.standfirst: The cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak​​ has docked at the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection, wrapping up a troubled journey that put world health authorities on alert.
items.content: The cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak​​ has docked at the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection, wrapping up a troubled journey that put world health authorities on alert.

items.title: Cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak docks in Rotterdam
items.standfirst: The ship's crew are expected to self-isolate after arriving at the Dutch port, their final destination.
items.content: The ship's crew are expected to self-isolate after arriving at the Dutch port, their final destination.

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship docks in the Netherlands
items.standfirst: MV Hondius was carrying 25 crew members and two medical personnel as it reached the Dutch port of Rotterdam.
items.content: MV Hondius was carrying 25 crew members and two medical personnel as it reached the Dutch port of Rotterdam.

items.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
items.standfirst: Health officials have identified at least 10 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.
items.content: Health officials have identified at least 10 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

items.title: Hantavirus ship arrives in Rotterdam for disinfection
items.standfirst: The cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak has arrived in the Netherlands and entered the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection.
items.content: The cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak has arrived in the Netherlands and entered the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection.

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrives in Netherlands, ending long and deadly voyage
items.standfirst: The hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrived Monday in the Netherlands, ending a seven-week, 8,500 mile journey that was hit by up to 11 infections and three deaths from the rare disease
items.content: The hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrived Monday in the Netherlands, ending a seven-week, 8,500 mile journey that was hit by up to 11 infections and three deaths from the rare disease

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius arrives in Rotterdam for disinfection
items.standfirst: The hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius on Monday reached the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection as authorities prepared quarantine arrangements for remaining crew and medical staff following an outbreak that has killed three people and triggered global monitoring.
items.content: The hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius on Monday reached the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection as authorities prepared quarantine arrangements for remaining crew and medical staff following an outbreak that has killed three people and triggered global monitoring.

items.title: Nine UK citizens exposed to hantavirus returning to Britain
items.standfirst: Nine people connected to the MV Hondius cruise ship, which was hit by a hantavirus outbreak, are set to arrive in the UK.
items.content: Nine people connected to the MV Hondius cruise ship, which was hit by a hantavirus outbreak, are set to arrive in the UK.

clusters.title: Fighter jets collide at US air show
clusters.neutral_headline: Idaho air force base locked down after midair collision, officials say
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian World, CBS News and others

items.title: Fighter jets collide at US air show
items.standfirst: Two #US fighter #jets collided midair while performing at an air show in #Idaho on Sunday, with all four crew members able to eject and parachute to safety. An investigation is underway to establish how the two US Navy EA-18 Growler fighter jets managed to come into contact with each other at the Gunfighter Skies Air Show.
items.content: Two #US fighter #jets collided midair while performing at an air show in #Idaho on Sunday, with all four crew members able to eject and parachute to safety. An investigation is underway to establish how the two US Navy EA-18 Growler fighter jets managed to come into contact with each other at the Gunfighter Skies Air Show.

items.title: Idaho air force base locked down after midair collision, officials say
items.standfirst: Four crew members are safe after incident involving aircraft during weekend air show at military base in western IdahoFour crew members are safe after two navy jets collided and crashed to the ground on Sunday at an air show at Mountain Home air force base in western Idaho, officials said.The collision involved two US navy EA-18G Growlers from the Electronic Attack Squadron 129 in Whidbey Island, Washington, said Cmdr Amelia Umayam, a spokesperson for Naval Air Forces, US Pacific Fleet. Continue reading...
items.content: Four crew members are safe after incident involving aircraft during weekend air show at military base in western IdahoFour crew members are safe after two navy jets collided and crashed to the ground on Sunday at an air show at Mountain Home air force base in western Idaho, officials said.The collision involved two US navy EA-18G Growlers from the Electronic Attack Squadron 129 in Whidbey Island, Washington, said Cmdr Amelia Umayam, a spokesperson for Naval Air Forces, US Pacific Fleet. Continue reading...

items.title: 5/17: CBS Weekend News
items.standfirst: Fighter jets collide in midair at Idaho air show; Trump's approval ratings on the economy continue to fall in latest CBS News poll.
items.content: Fighter jets collide in midair at Idaho air show; Trump's approval ratings on the economy continue to fall in latest CBS News poll.

items.title: Moment of collision between two Navy jets at Idaho air show
items.standfirst: Two US Navy jets collided during an air show at Mountain Home AFB.
items.content: Two US Navy jets collided during an air show at Mountain Home AFB.

items.title: Trump's approval ratings on the economy continue to fall in latest CBS News poll
items.standfirst: President Trump's approval rating is now at the lowest level of his second term and only 27% of Americans approve of how he is handling inflation, according to a new CBS News poll. Olivia Rinaldi reports.
items.content: President Trump's approval rating is now at the lowest level of his second term and only 27% of Americans approve of how he is handling inflation, according to a new CBS News poll. Olivia Rinaldi reports.

items.title: Fighter jets collide in midair at Idaho air show
items.standfirst: Two fighter jets collided in midair Sunday during an air show in Idaho. Officials say the crewmembers are in stable condition after they ejected.
items.content: Two fighter jets collided in midair Sunday during an air show in Idaho. Officials say the crewmembers are in stable condition after they ejected.

items.title: Moment two fighter jets collide mid-air at US air show
items.standfirst: Four crew members safely ejected from US Navy jets and are in a stable condition after the collision.
items.content: Four crew members safely ejected from US Navy jets and are in a stable condition after the collision.

items.title: Four crew members ejected after military jets crash during US air show
items.standfirst: All four crew members were safely ejected after two navy jets crashed during an air show at Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho.
items.content: All four crew members were safely ejected after two navy jets crashed during an air show at Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho.

clusters.title: Two dozen injured in Ukraine following Russian strikes
clusters.neutral_headline: At least four people killed in Russia as Ukraine launches retaliatory strikes
bullet_summary: Ukrainian authorities said that the two civilian vessels hit included one ‌under the Marshall Islands flag and another under the Guinea-Bissau flag
At least four people were killed, including three in the Moscow region, after Ukraine launched its biggest overnight drone attack on the Russian capital in more ​than a year, local officials said
Four people were killed in overnight Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia, authorities have said – in what appears to be the largest attack on Moscow in over a year

items.title: Two dozen injured in Ukraine following Russian strikes
items.standfirst: Russia targeted eight regions of Ukraine in its latest nighttime drone and missile barrage, with local authorities reporting that the strikes wounded more than two dozen civilians, including three children. The city of Dnipro bore the brunt of the attack, which saw more than 500 hundred drones launched against the central region of the country.
items.content: Russia targeted eight regions of Ukraine in its latest nighttime drone and missile barrage, with local authorities reporting that the strikes wounded more than two dozen civilians, including three children. The city of Dnipro bore the brunt of the attack, which saw more than 500 hundred drones launched against the central region of the country.

items.title: Russia and Ukraine exchange overnight drone attacks
items.standfirst: Russia launched hundreds of drones and nearly two dozen missiles at Ukraine overnight wounding more than 30 people in the cities of Odesa and Dnipro. A Russian drone also hit a Chinese cargo ship in the Black Sea, a day before Russian President Vladmimir Putin is meeting with Xi Jinping in Beijing. At least four died in Russia following Ukraine's biggest overnight drone attack on Moscow in more than a year. FRANCE 24's Emmanuelle Chaze tells us more from Kharkiv.
items.content: Russia launched hundreds of drones and nearly two dozen missiles at Ukraine overnight wounding more than 30 people in the cities of Odesa and Dnipro. A Russian drone also hit a Chinese cargo ship in the Black Sea, a day before Russian President Vladmimir Putin is meeting with Xi Jinping in Beijing. At least four died in Russia following Ukraine's biggest overnight drone attack on Moscow in more than a year. FRANCE 24's Emmanuelle Chaze tells us more from Kharkiv.

items.title: Russian drones hit two ships off Ukraine's coast, including Chinese-owned vessel
items.standfirst: Russia launched hundreds of drones and nearly two dozen missiles at Ukraine overnight, hitting ​two ships in the Black Sea near Ukraine's Odesa region and wounding more than 30 people. Ukrainian authorities said that the two civilian vessels hit included one ‌under the Marshall Islands flag and another under the Guinea-Bissau flag.
items.content: Russia launched hundreds of drones and nearly two dozen missiles at Ukraine overnight, hitting ​two ships in the Black Sea near Ukraine's Odesa region and wounding more than 30 people. Ukrainian authorities said that the two civilian vessels hit included one ‌under the Marshall Islands flag and another under the Guinea-Bissau flag.

items.title: At least four people killed in Russia as Ukraine launches retaliatory strikes
items.standfirst: Wave of almost 600 drones launched across 14 regions, after Moscow’s deadly three-day attack on Ukraine last weekOne of Ukraine’s largest ever drone strikes against Russia’s regions, including Moscow, has killed at least four people and wounded a dozen more, the Russian authorities have said.The wave of almost 600 Ukrainian drones struck overnight across 14 Russian regions, as well as the Crimean peninsula and the Black and Azov seas, the Russian defence ministry said on Sunday, with the area around the capital among the worst-hit. Continue reading...
items.content: Wave of almost 600 drones launched across 14 regions, after Moscow’s deadly three-day attack on Ukraine last weekOne of Ukraine’s largest ever drone strikes against Russia’s regions, including Moscow, has killed at least four people and wounded a dozen more, the Russian authorities have said.The wave of almost 600 Ukrainian drones struck overnight across 14 Russian regions, as well as the Crimean peninsula and the Black and Azov seas, the Russian defence ministry said on Sunday, with the area around the capital among the worst-hit. Continue reading...

items.title: Large-scale Ukrainian drone attack kills three in Moscow region, says Russia
items.standfirst: Ukraine's president says the strikes are a "justified" response to deadly Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities.
items.content: Ukraine's president says the strikes are a "justified" response to deadly Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities.

items.title: Ukraine drones kill four in Russia; Moscow faces biggest attack in over a year
items.standfirst: At least four people were killed, including three in the Moscow region, after Ukraine launched its biggest overnight drone attack on the Russian capital in more ​than a year, local officials said.
items.content: At least four people were killed, including three in the Moscow region, after Ukraine launched its biggest overnight drone attack on the Russian capital in more ​than a year, local officials said.

items.title: Indian worker among four killed as Ukraine launches largest drone attack on Moscow in over a year
items.standfirst: Four people were killed in overnight Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia, authorities have said – in what appears to be the largest attack on Moscow in over a year.
items.content: Four people were killed in overnight Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia, authorities have said – in what appears to be the largest attack on Moscow in over a year.

clusters.title: DR Congo takes emergency measures to contain new Ebola outbreak
clusters.neutral_headline: Uganda responds with emergency measures to Ebola outbreak
bullet_summary: Cases have also been reported in neighbouring Uganda, among travellers from Congo
At least 80 deaths have been reported in a new Ebola disease outbreak in Congo and Uganda, authorities said
An outbreak in DR Congo has been declared a health emergency, with almost 250 suspected cases reported

items.title: DR Congo takes emergency measures to contain new Ebola outbreak
items.standfirst: DRC authorities have been advised to take emergency measures in a bid to contain the latest Ebola outbreak. Dozens of lives have already been claimed by the outbreak, which has been declared a public health emergency of international concern by the World health Organisation. Cases have also been reported in neighbouring Uganda, among travellers from Congo.
items.content: DRC authorities have been advised to take emergency measures in a bid to contain the latest Ebola outbreak. Dozens of lives have already been claimed by the outbreak, which has been declared a public health emergency of international concern by the World health Organisation. Cases have also been reported in neighbouring Uganda, among travellers from Congo.

items.title: Uganda responds with emergency measures to Ebola outbreak
items.standfirst: Al Jazeera’s Catherine Soi reports on the emergency measures implemented in Uganda in response to the Ebola outbreak.
items.content: Al Jazeera’s Catherine Soi reports on the emergency measures implemented in Uganda in response to the Ebola outbreak.

items.title: At least 6 Americans in Congo were exposed to Ebola virus, sources say
items.standfirst: The World Health Organization this week declared the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a "public health emergency of international concern."
items.content: The World Health Organization this week declared the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a "public health emergency of international concern."

items.title: Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda declared global health emergency
items.standfirst: At least 80 deaths have been reported in a new Ebola disease outbreak in Congo and Uganda, authorities said.
items.content: At least 80 deaths have been reported in a new Ebola disease outbreak in Congo and Uganda, authorities said.

items.title: Ebola outbreak declared a global health emergency by WHO
items.standfirst: Outbreaks of the deadly Ebola virus in two African countries have prompted the World Health Organization to declare a global health emergency. Imtiaz Tyab reports.
items.content: Outbreaks of the deadly Ebola virus in two African countries have prompted the World Health Organization to declare a global health emergency. Imtiaz Tyab reports.

items.title: How worrying is the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo?
items.standfirst: An outbreak in DR Congo has been declared a health emergency, with almost 250 suspected cases reported.
items.content: An outbreak in DR Congo has been declared a health emergency, with almost 250 suspected cases reported.

clusters.title: Pop star Shakira is acquitted in a Spanish tax fraud case
clusters.neutral_headline: Pop star Shakira is acquitted in a Spanish tax fraud case
bullet_summary: Spanish tax authorities did not prove that the singer was a resident of Spain, the court said in its decision
Reported by NPR News, Sky News World

items.title: Pop star Shakira is acquitted in a Spanish tax fraud case
items.standfirst: The decision follows years of tax troubles in Spain for the Colombian superstar. Spanish tax authorities did not prove that the singer was a resident of Spain, the court said in its decision.
items.content: The decision follows years of tax troubles in Spain for the Colombian superstar. Spanish tax authorities did not prove that the singer was a resident of Spain, the court said in its decision.(Image credit: Charles Sykes)

items.title: Shakira acquitted of tax fraud
items.standfirst: Singer Shakira has been acquitted of tax fraud and should be reimbursed more than 55m euros (£48m), Spain's High Court has ruled, following years of financial legal battles.
items.content: Singer Shakira has been acquitted of tax fraud and should be reimbursed more than 55m euros (£48m), Spain's High Court has ruled, following years of financial legal battles.

clusters.title: French cinema rocked by blacklist threat from biggest producer
clusters.neutral_headline: French cinema rocked by blacklist threat from biggest producer

items.title: French cinema rocked by blacklist threat from biggest producer
items.standfirst: It is a shock threat to Europe's film industry. The head of France's biggest film producer, Canal Plus, is saying it will blacklist 600 film industry professionals who have signed a petition against the right-wing billionaire owner Vincent Bolloré. The announcement was made in Cannes in southern France where the world's movie elite is gathered for the Cannes Film Festival.
items.content: It is a shock threat to Europe's film industry. The head of France's biggest film producer, Canal Plus, is saying it will blacklist 600 film industry professionals who have signed a petition against the right-wing billionaire owner Vincent Bolloré. The announcement was made in Cannes in southern France where the world's movie elite is gathered for the Cannes Film Festival.

items.title: France’s top film producer says it will blacklist figures who petitioned against rightwing billionaire
items.standfirst: Canal+ head says he will not work with hundreds of actors and directors who signed protest against Vincent Bolloré’s political swayThe head of France’s biggest film producer, Canal+, has said the group will no longer work with hundreds of cinema figures who signed a petition voicing concern over the growing influence of the rightwing billionaire owner Vincent Bolloré.The open letter, published earlier this week to coincide with the opening of the Cannes film festival, was signed by more than 600 figures, including the actor-director Juliette Binoche, the director and photographer Raymond Depardon, the French-Iranian film-maker Sepideh Farsi and the director Arthur Harari, who co-wrote the Oscar-winning Anatomy of a Fall and is premiering his film The Unknown in the main competition in Cannes. Continue reading...
items.content: Canal+ head says he will not work with hundreds of actors and directors who signed protest against Vincent Bolloré’s political swayThe head of France’s biggest film producer, Canal+, has said the group will no longer work with hundreds of cinema figures who signed a petition voicing concern over the growing influence of the rightwing billionaire owner Vincent Bolloré.The open letter, published earlier this week to coincide with the opening of the Cannes film festival, was signed by more than 600 figures, including the actor-director Juliette Binoche, the director and photographer Raymond Depardon, the French-Iranian film-maker Sepideh Farsi and the director Arthur Harari, who co-wrote the Oscar-winning Anatomy of a Fall and is premiering his film The Unknown in the main competition in Cannes. Continue reading...

items.title: Aaron Rai becomes first English winner of PGA Championship in over a century
items.standfirst: England's Aaron Rai has claimed his first major championship by winning the US PGA Championship - and becomes the first English winner of the event in more than a century.
items.content: England's Aaron Rai has claimed his first major championship by winning the US PGA Championship - and becomes the first English winner of the event in more than a century.

items.title: Rai first Englishman to win US PGA Championship since 1919
items.standfirst: England's Aaron Rai wins the first major title of his career with a magnificent final-round 65 in the US PGA Championship at Aronimink.
